page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.48xlarge is an ARM64-based compute-optimized server powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.7 GHz. The architecture features 192 vCPUs with a single thread per core, 384.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core, and a built-in 11400 GB NVMe SSD local storage array. Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression tests all scoring in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also highly competitive, placing in the top 10% for both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read/write operations. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the middle 50% average category.

The server demonstrates strong database and web serving capabilities, achieving top-tier results in Passmark database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. Large-scale prompt processing for 7B parameter language models is strong, though prompt processing for smaller 135M models is weak. Given the high physical core density and integrated NVMe storage, this instance offers high resource density for multi-threaded compute workloads, high-throughput web serving, and distributed database systems, though it presents a tradeoff in single-threaded execution speed.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.48xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores operating at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM and 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, this instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Conversely, single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks fall into the average category. The server is suited for highly parallelized workloads, including high-throughput web serving, Redis databases, and multi-threaded data processing, offering high resource density and local storage capacity at the expense of peak single-threaded speed.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.48xlarge is an ARM64 server with 192 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 11400 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.48xlarge is an ARM64 server with 192 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 11,400 GB NVMe SSD storage for multi-core workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized platform featuring 192 dedicated physical cores, high-capacity local NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core and database throughput.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 physical cores at 2.7 GHz.
384.0 GB of system memory and 11400 GB of integrated NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis database performance.
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Suited for highly parallelized workloads, static web serving, and database operations.
